seo.title: Pumpkin Slices - a great treat - Marinda Kook

html:
I think I've already posted this recipe on my blog before, but it's so delicious and easy that I'm putting it up again. What's great about the recipe is that you put it in the oven and then just forget about it. The pumpkin doesn't just taste good, it looks lovely too. Such a nice rustic look!

 

YOU WILL NEED:

  • 6 slices of pumpkin – any kind your heart desires
  • 175 ml honey – or however much your hand feels like pouring
  • 100 ml olive oil
  • 125 ml cinnamon sugar
  • 100 ml sunflower seeds – or seeds of your choice
  • 4 sprigs of thyme

NOW:

  1. cut the pumpkin into slices as thick as you like
  2. place the slices in an oven dish
  3. sprinkle with the cinnamon sugar
  4. pour the olive oil and honey over ( I always warm the honey in the microwave so it's nice and runny)
  5. sprinkle the seeds over
  6. place the thyme on the pumpkin
  7. bake for 40 minutes at 180 degrees Celsius in the oven, uncovered – open
  8. turn the pumpkin after 20 minutes
  9. the thickness of your slices will determine how long it takes in the oven – feel with a fork whether it's soft

Faithful pumpkin – always a winner! The syrup gets so lovely and syrupy and sweeeeeeeeet.
